decision: do_not_try_to_force_all_metrics_above_0_95; rescue only metrics that are scientifically plausible and paper-critical

threshold_policy.hard_rule: Do not present component metrics below 0.95 as solved or final if the manuscript wording implies near-perfect performance.
threshold_policy.paper_strategy: Either rescue with bounded experiments, move to diagnostic/negative evidence, or change the claim to an operating-point/ablation claim.
threshold_policy.warning: For detection tasks, F1/precision/recall above 0.95 is likely unrealistic on current raster outputs; forcing this before 2026-05-23 risks invalid claims.

items.current: 0.926172 primary dev; 0.918303 secondary smoke; candidate is 1.000000
items.issue: baseline below 0.95 but candidate already exceeds threshold
items.action: Use candidate 1.0 as positive result; do not headline baseline; keep expected-json replay caveat. No model work needed.

items.issue: far below 0.95; cannot be claimed as high-accuracy recognition
items.action: Reframe as operating-point/inflation reduction, not final recognition. If rescue attempted, target center recall/inflation tradeoff and subset qualitative evidence, not >0.95 F1.
Keep as diagnostic/ablation evidence; do not headline as solved room extraction. Optional quick rescue: tune mask-to-box postprocess for center recall, but not expected to reach 0.95.
Run threshold/top-k sweep on existing predictions only; optimize center recall >=0.90 with inflation cap. Stop if no improvement in <2h.

items.issue: ranking signal below 0.95 AP but AUC usable
items.action: Do not claim selector success. Optional: calibrate/listwise budget sweep using existing predictions to reduce inflation; success criterion gate pass, not 0.95 AP.

items.issue: one submetric already >0.95, but precision poor
items.action: If needed, present high recall only with precision caveat; optional threshold sweep for balanced F1, not solved claim.
Keep as negative evidence; remove from main positive claims.
Appendix diagnostics only or omit from main paper.
Negative evidence; no further hand-tuning unless needed for future work.

proposed_next_tasks.goal: Patch manuscript language so no metric below 0.95 is framed as solved/high-accuracy; keep P095 candidate 1.0 as bounded replay result.
Use existing symbol predictions only to see whether a center-recall/inflation operating point can be improved quickly; no long training.
Check existing image-only room masks for a better precision/recall/F1 threshold; stop if no strong gain.
Update final tables/abstract after rescue attempts; demote failed metrics to appendix/negative evidence.

success_criteria.must: No paper sentence implies >0.95-quality for metrics that are below 0.95.
success_criteria.ideal: Find at least one additional bounded submetric above 0.90 or a cleaner operating point for symbols/rooms.
success_criteria.stop_condition: If only marginal gains are found, prioritize manuscript honesty over metric chasing.
